reason: Backend MCP tool additions and validation rules apply to new Node-side tool registration and transport behavior.
New path and class-related inputs must keep the existing safety contract for Godot-side operations.
New tools must preserve structured error behavior at the MCP boundary.
TypeScript implementation and validation changes should follow the repo's backend quality bar.
The Godot plugin layer is stored under frontend specs and governs editor/plugin-side changes.
Plugin-side validation for signals, scenes, and input map mutation must remain explicit and safe.
GDScript-side implementation should follow the plugin quality bar.
This roadmap explains the product boundary and why richer capabilities matter more than hard-coded workflows.
